title: Gap Closure, Deliberate Practice, and Honest Positioning · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed hiring review reconstructs gap closure, deliberate practice, and honest positioning from target, claim, evidence, audience, action, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, consent, owner, and next-decision evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.
Design the smallest authentic task that can reveal or improve one missing behavior under a changed case.
Precommit a time, evidence, and opportunity-cost stop rule for readiness work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Apply Learn Loop
steps.instruction:
review case — Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

Inspect the Apply Learn Loop trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Apply Learn Loop.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Apply Learn Loop, keep Apply Learn Loop connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Apply Learn Loop, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Apply Learn Loop, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Apply Learn Loop precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Apply Learn Loop, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Apply Learn Loop, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: All application outcomes identify the exact skill gap to fix.

stimulus.title: Apply Learn Loop case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The review variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: All application outcomes identify the exact skill gap to fix.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise. Artifact under review: time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Apply Learn Loop.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Apply Learn Loop, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
